name: Replicate API
what_it_does: Replicate's API for running open-source machine learning models in the cloud, including image generation, LLMs, audio processing, and computer vision models via simple API calls.
use_cases: Running Stable Diffusion and other image generation models from agents
Inference with open-source LLMs (Llama, Mistral, etc.) via simple API
Video generation and processing via cloud GPU models
Audio transcription and generation using hosted models
Rapid prototyping with diverse ML models without GPU infrastructure
not_for: Production serving of models at high throughput (latency is variable)
Fine-tuning models at scale (better done with dedicated ML platforms)
Teams needing data privacy guarantees (inputs are sent to Replicate)
Sub-100ms inference requirements (cold start latency applies)
best_when: An agent needs access to diverse open-source ML models for image, audio, or text processing without managing GPU infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need guaranteed low latency, model privacy, or high-throughput production inference.

auth.notes: Simple API token authentication. Token from Replicate account settings. Full account access per token. No scopes. Very easy for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free credits for new accounts ($5-10 typically)
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay per prediction based on GPU compute time. Varies by model.
pricing.notes: Pay per model run based on GPU seconds. Pricing varies significantly by model — image generation ~$0.0023 per run, LLMs per token. Credit card required after free credits.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on plan, typically 10-50 concurrent predictions

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include detail field with human-readable messages. Model-specific errors are surfaced clearly. Timeout errors and cold start warnings are well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each prediction creates a new run. No idempotency keys. Agents should track prediction IDs to avoid duplicate runs.
known_agent_gotchas: Predictions are asynchronous by default — agents must poll for completion or use webhooks
Cold start latency can add 10-60 seconds for infrequently used models
Model versions can be deprecated — agents must pin to specific version IDs
Input schema varies significantly per model — agents must check model's schema
Large file outputs (images, video) are URLs that expire after 1 hour
Concurrent prediction limits apply — agents running many parallel predictions may be throttled
